nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving streams and shaded freshwater bodies in Sri Lanka; typically grows along shaded margins of rainforest streams where it can experience seasonal emersed/submerged transitions.

decorPreferences: plant in small groups around rocks or driftwood

avoidWith: Silver Dollars and other plant-eating fish

summary: A small Sri Lankan crypt with narrow olive-green leaves; well suited to foreground or midground placement. Beginner-friendly and tolerant of low light, but prone to classic crypt melt during acclimation.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne lucens is a small rosette crypt native to streams in Sri Lanka, generally considered one of the more compact members of the genus and well suited to foreground or midground use. Modern Aquarium describes it as remaining only around 5 cm tall in the aquarium, while Aquarium Co-Op cites roughly 7-8 cm — we have used a height range of approximately 5-10 cm to span both reports. The leaves are narrow and lance-shaped, ranging from bright green to olive-green depending on light intensity. The species spreads by runners (stolons) and, once established, can form a loose dense bushy carpet, though growth is slow and patience is required; planting multiple specimens close together is the conventional way to fill in a foreground more quickly. Lighting tolerance is broad: it has been reported to thrive in low light (Aquarium Co-Op) and also accepts moderate lighting (Modern Aquarium). CO2 is not required but the plant tends to respond well to it with faster growth and richer color. As with all crypts, it is a heavy root feeder — plant it in nutrient-rich aquarium substrate and supplement with root tabs in the root zone for best results. Plant by burying the roots up to (but NOT including) the crown where the leaves emerge from the rhizome; burying the crown causes rot. CRYPT MELT WARNING: like other Cryptocoryne species, C. lucens is well known for crypt melt — after planting, water-change shocks, or major parameter shifts the plant will frequently shed most or all of its leaves over a few days. This is a normal acclimation response and the rhizome should be left in place; the plant generally regrows new leaves adapted to the new conditions within 2-4 weeks. Stable parameters are the best way to minimize melt. Cryptocoryne lucens is commonly confused with Cryptocoryne willisii (and sometimes with the smaller Cryptocoryne parva, per Modern Aquarium), and trade specimens may be mislabeled between these species. INFERRED VALUES (no explicit source confirmation): water-parameter ranges (temperature 22-28 °C, pH 6.0-7.5, gH 2-15, kH 1-18), CO2 as optional, fertilization as moderate, substrate as nutrient-rich, and minimum tank size (40 L / 40 cm length) are inferred from Cryptocoryne genus defaults and from the closely-related Cryptocoryne walkeri profile; Modern Aquarium gave a temperature range of 68-82 °F (~20-28 °C) which aligns with this. Aquarium Co-Op and Modern Aquarium were both used as research sources; both agreed on Sri Lankan origin (implied/explicit), small size, slow growth, and foreground/midground use. Sources disagreed on lighting: Modern Aquarium said moderate, Aquarium Co-Op said low — we have set the typed plant.lighting field to 'low' (matching the broader Cryptocoryne genus consensus and Aquarium Co-Op) and noted the moderate-light tolerance here.
breedingNotes: Propagated primarily by runners (stolons) that produce daughter plants alongside the parent; once a daughter plant has developed several leaves and its own root system it can be carefully separated from the runner and replanted elsewhere. Rhizome division is also possible: cut a healthy rhizome into sections, each retaining leaves and roots, and replant. Like other crypts, Cryptocoryne lucens can flower (producing the spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of Araceae) when grown emersed, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Confirmed Sri Lankan origin, small size (~2 inches in the aquarium), foreground placement, moderate lighting tolerance, temperature 68-82 °F (~20-28 °C), easy/adaptable care level, and confusion with Cryptocoryne parva.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked slow growth, low-light tolerance, ~3 inches tall at maturity, foreground-to-midground placement, runner-based spreading habit, and dense narrow bright-green-leaved carpeting behavior. Disagreed with Modern Aquarium on lighting (low vs moderate); both reads are reasonable and we set the typed lighting field to 'low' per genus consensus.
